message:
fix(agent): restart-backend resolves real port + console binary (#7945) (#7983)

* fix(agent): restart-backend resolves real port + console binary (#7945)

The /restart-backend endpoint (kc-agent, pkg/agent/server_http.go) had
three bugs under the watchdog architecture (cmd/console/watchdog.go):

1. killBackendProcess hard-coded `lsof -ti :8080 -sTCP:LISTEN`. In
   watchdog deployments port 8080 is the watchdog reverse proxy, not
   the backend — so the fallback killed the watchdog and left the real
   backend (on 8081) running. Now resolves the port via BACKEND_PORT,
   the watchdog PID file, or the legacy 8080 default.

2. startBackendProcess re-execed os.Executable(), which in the kc-agent
   process returns the kc-agent binary, not cmd/console. That spawned a
   second kc-agent (which then failed to bind 8585) instead of
   restarting the backend. Now locates the `console` binary via
   KC_CONSOLE_BINARY, a sibling of os.Executable(), or $PATH, and
   returns an error instead of silently re-execing the wrong binary.
   BACKEND_PORT is injected into the child env, deduped.

3. checkBackendHealth and waitForBackendHealth hit the hard-coded
   http://127.0.0.1:8080/health. Now both use the resolved port so a
   watchdog-mode restart actually checks the backend, not the proxy.

selfUpdateFallback (pkg/agent/update_checker.go) shares the kill/
restart callbacks via UpdateCheckerConfig, so the brew-install
auto-update path benefits from the same fix without further changes.

Adds unit tests for resolveBackendPort (env/PID/legacy branches),
backendHealthURL, and envWithBackendPort (dedupe guarantee).

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* ci: update-guard invariant grep matches refactored defer cleanup

The invariant check was grepping for the literal `defer atomic.StoreInt32`,
but `checkForUpdates` was refactored to use a local `cleanup` closure that
is deferred from each start path:

  cleanup := func() {
      atomic.StoreInt32(&uc.updating, 0)
      ...
  }
  ...
  defer cleanup()

The functional guarantee (the updating flag is cleared on every exit) still
holds, but the literal grep no longer matches and reports a false failure
on any PR that touches pkg/agent/update_checker.go. Main has been failing
this check silently — the workflow only triggers on PRs that edit one of a
small set of files, so most PRs never saw it.

Update the grep to verify the real invariant: the cleanup closure clears
the flag AND at least one `defer cleanup()` call site exists.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -83,8 +83,13 @@ jobs:
             echo "PASS: Atomic CAS for mutual exclusion present"
           fi
 
-          # 2. Defer cleanup of updating flag
-          if ! grep -q "defer atomic.StoreInt32" "$CHECKER"; then
+          # 2. Defer cleanup of updating flag.
+          # The cleanup was refactored from an inline `defer atomic.StoreInt32`
+          # into a local `cleanup := func() { atomic.StoreInt32(&uc.updating, 0); … }`
+          # closure that's deferred from every start path. Verify both:
+          # (a) cleanup closure exists and clears the updating flag, and
+          # (b) at least one `defer cleanup()` call site exists.
+          if ! grep -q "atomic.StoreInt32(&uc.updating, 0)" "$CHECKER" || ! grep -q "defer cleanup()" "$CHECKER"; then
             echo "FAIL: Missing defer cleanup of updating flag"
             ERRORS=$((ERRORS + 1))
           else

@@ -31,10 +31,21 @@ const (
 	stabilizationDelay    = 3 * time.Second
 	startupDelay          = 500 * time.Millisecond
 	metricsHistoryTick    = 10 * time.Minute
-	agentFileMode         = 0600
-	defaultHealthCheckURL = "http://127.0.0.1:8080/health"
-	maxQueryLimit         = 1000    // Upper bound for client-supplied limit query parameter
-	maxRequestBodyBytes   = 1 << 20 // 1MB upper bound for request body reads
+	agentFileMode = 0600
+
+	// Backend port resolution constants (see resolveBackendPort in server_http.go).
+	// These are duplicated from cmd/console/watchdog.go because pkg/agent cannot
+	// import the main package. Keep them in sync with watchdog.go.
+	backendPortWatchdogMode  = 8081               // watchdog (8080) proxies -> backend (8081)
+	backendPortLegacyDefault = 8080               // no-watchdog deployments: backend binds 8080 directly
+	watchdogPidFilePath      = "/tmp/.kc-watchdog.pid"
+	backendHealthScheme      = "http"
+	backendHealthHost        = "127.0.0.1"
+	backendHealthPath        = "/health"
+	backendPortEnvVar        = "BACKEND_PORT"
+
+	maxQueryLimit       = 1000    // Upper bound for client-supplied limit query parameter
+	maxRequestBodyBytes = 1 << 20 // 1MB upper bound for request body reads
 
 	// missionExecutionTimeout is the maximum wall-clock time a single mission
 	// chat execution (AI provider call) is allowed to run before the context
